_meta.description: Human-curated paper statements for Bridgeland (2007), 'Stability conditions on triangulated categories'. This file is the single source of truth for row-level data (prose, section, title, kind) keyed by paper reference. It is joined at dashboard-generation time with the `paperRef` field on `@[informal]` attributes in the project. The file knows nothing about Lean.
_meta._note_draft: SEED DRAFT — requires human review before merging. Prose is seeded from the LLM-generated `prose` field in informalizations.json for each currently tagged declaration, lightly edited for row-level voice. Every entry should be checked against the paper (math/0212237v3) for fidelity. Status does not appear in this file: per-declaration coverage status (`complete` | `partial`) lives on the `@[informal]` attribute as a separate field (added in a follow-up PR to lean-informal). Row-level status is derived at dashboard-generation time from the tagged decls' statuses.

kind.definition: A Definition environment in the paper.

sections.8: A generalized metric on the space of stability conditions

statements.title: Numerical stability conditions form a complex manifold
statements.prose: Each connected component of the space of numerical stability conditions on a triangulated category 𝒟 of finite type (satisfying the standard numerical finiteness hypotheses) carries the structure of a finite-dimensional complex manifold, modelled on a linear subspace of Hom(N(𝒟), ℂ).

statements.title: Stability function
statements.prose: A stability function on an abelian category 𝒜 is a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒜) → ℂ such that for every nonzero object E ∈ 𝒜, the complex number Z(E) lies in the semi-closed upper half plane { r · exp(iπφ) : r > 0, 0 < φ ≤ 1 }.

statements.title: Semistable object
statements.prose: A nonzero object E ∈ 𝒜 is semistable with respect to a stability function Z if every nonzero subobject A ↪ E satisfies φ(A) ≤ φ(E), where φ denotes the phase determined by Z.

statements.title: Harder–Narasimhan filtration
statements.prose: A Harder–Narasimhan filtration of a nonzero object E ∈ 𝒜 with respect to a stability function Z is a finite chain of subobjects 0 = E₀ ⊂ E₁ ⊂ ⋯ ⊂ Eₙ = E whose successive quotients Fᵢ = Eᵢ / Eᵢ₋₁ are semistable with strictly decreasing phases φ(F₁) > φ(F₂) > ⋯ > φ(Fₙ). The stability function Z has the HN property if every nonzero object admits such a filtration.

statements.title: Phase bounds from distinguished triangles
statements.prose: Let A → E → B → A[1] be a distinguished triangle in a triangulated category with slicing 𝒫, and suppose the phases of A and B lie in a common interval (a, b) with b − a ≤ 1. Then the maximum and minimum phases of E are bracketed by those of A and B: φ⁺(A) ≤ φ⁺(E) ≤ φ⁺(B) and φ⁻(A) ≤ φ⁻(E) ≤ φ⁻(B).

statements.title: Slicing of a triangulated category
statements.prose: A slicing 𝒫 of a triangulated category 𝒯 assigns to each φ ∈ ℝ a full additive subcategory 𝒫(φ) ⊂ 𝒯 satisfying: (a) 𝒫(φ + 1) = 𝒫(φ)[1]; (b) if φ₁ > φ₂ and Aⱼ ∈ 𝒫(φⱼ), then Hom_𝒯(A₁, A₂) = 0; (c) every nonzero object admits a Harder–Narasimhan filtration — a finite sequence of distinguished triangles 0 = E₀ → E₁ → ⋯ → Eₙ = E whose successive cones Aᵢ lie in 𝒫(φᵢ) for strictly decreasing phases φ₁ > φ₂ > ⋯ > φₙ.

statements.title: Quasi-abelian category
statements.prose: A preadditive category with kernels, cokernels, pullbacks, and pushouts is quasi-abelian if strict monomorphisms are stable under pushout and strict epimorphisms are stable under pullback. This is the definition used by Schneiders (1999), underlying Bridgeland's treatment of hearts of t-structures.

statements.title: Skewed stability function
statements.prose: A skewed stability function on a thin subcategory 𝒫((a, b)) consists of a group homomorphism W : Λ → ℂ (the perturbed charge) and a skewing parameter α ∈ (a, b), such that W(v[E]) lies in a strict half-plane determined by α for every nonzero object E of the subcategory. The skewed stability function is used to express small perturbations of a central charge while keeping track of the phase window they affect.

statements.title: Stability condition
statements.prose: A stability condition σ = (Z, 𝒫) on a triangulated category 𝒟 consists of a group homomorphism Z : K(𝒟) → ℂ (the central charge) and a slicing 𝒫 of 𝒟, such that for every nonzero E ∈ 𝒫(φ) one has Z(E) = m(E) · exp(iπφ) for some m(E) > 0.

statements.title: Stability conditions as bounded t-structures with stability functions
statements.prose: Giving a stability condition on a triangulated category 𝒟 is equivalent to giving a bounded t-structure on 𝒟 whose heart 𝒜 carries a stability function with the Harder–Narasimhan property. The correspondence sends a stability condition σ = (Z, 𝒫) to the bounded t-structure with heart 𝒫((0, 1]), and conversely a bounded t-structure with stability function Z on its heart extends uniquely to a stability condition on the ambient category.

statements.title: Phase slices are abelian
statements.prose: For any stability condition σ = (Z, 𝒫) on a triangulated category and any phase φ ∈ ℝ, the full subcategory 𝒫(φ) is an abelian category.

statements.title: Locally finite stability condition
statements.prose: A slicing 𝒫 is locally finite if there exists η > 0 with η < 1/2 such that every quasi-abelian subcategory 𝒫((t − η, t + η)) is of finite length, i.e. satisfies both ACC and DCC on strict subobjects. A stability condition is locally finite if its underlying slicing is.

statements.title: Generalized metric on slicings
statements.prose: The space of slicings of a triangulated category 𝒯 carries a generalized (left-invariant) metric d(𝒫, 𝒬), defined as the infimum of ε ≥ 0 such that 𝒫(φ) ⊂ 𝒬((φ − ε, φ + ε)) and 𝒬(φ) ⊂ 𝒫((φ − ε, φ + ε)) for every φ ∈ ℝ. Equivalently, d(𝒫, 𝒬) is the supremum over nonzero objects E of the phase differences max(|φ⁺_𝒫(E) − φ⁺_𝒬(E)|, |φ⁻_𝒫(E) − φ⁻_𝒬(E)|).

statements.title: Seminorm domination on connected components
statements.prose: If σ and τ lie in the same connected component of the space of locally finite stability conditions, then there exists a finite constant K such that ‖U‖_σ ≤ K · ‖U‖_τ for every U ∈ Hom(K(𝒟), ℂ). Applying this in both directions establishes that the stability seminorms of any two points in the same component are equivalent.

statements.title: Local injectivity of the central charge
statements.prose: Let σ, τ be stability conditions on 𝒟 with the same central charge Z and with d(𝒫, 𝒬) < 1. Then 𝒫 = 𝒬, and hence σ = τ. In particular, on each connected component of Stab(𝒟) the forgetful map sending a stability condition to its central charge is locally injective.

statements.title: Central charge is a local homeomorphism on connected components
statements.prose: For each connected component Σ of the space of locally finite stability conditions on a triangulated category 𝒟, there is a linear subspace V(Σ) ⊂ Hom(K(𝒟), ℂ) carrying a natural linear topology, such that the forgetful map Σ → V(Σ), σ ↦ Z(σ) sending a stability condition to its central charge is a local homeomorphism onto V(Σ).

statements.title: Deformation of stability conditions
statements.prose: Let σ = (Z, 𝒫) be a locally finite stability condition on a triangulated category 𝒟. Then there exists ε₀ > 0 such that for every group homomorphism W : K(𝒟) → ℂ with ‖W − Z‖_σ < sin(π ε₀), there is a locally finite stability condition τ = (W, 𝒬) with d(𝒫, 𝒬) < ε₀. In other words, small perturbations of the central charge lift uniquely to nearby stability conditions.
